title: Our Vehicles 
summary: Lotus, a prestigious British luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer with over 70 years of expertise in high-performance automotive design, is redefining the electric vehicle market with its innovative lifestyle offerings. The company's first production vehicle, the Eletre, is a state-of-the-art all-electric SUV that showcases advanced technology and performance, featuring a proprietary 800-volt EPA powertrain. Launched in 2023, the Eletre combines a sleek aerodynamic design with a luxurious interior and cutting-edge connectivity features, including a 15.1-inch OLED display and 5G compatibility for real-time updates. It offers three distinct versions—Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R—catering to varying customer preferences, with impressive acceleration and range capabilities.

Following the Eletre, Lotus introduced the Emeya, a four-door luxury hyper grand tourer (hyper-GT) that emphasizes sustainable performance and advanced technology. Set to begin deliveries in March 2024, the Emeya features rapid acceleration and supercharging capabilities, appealing to eco-conscious drivers who prioritize both luxury and performance. With plans for additional electric models, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV sports car in 2026, Lotus is committed to continuous innovation and sustainability in the automotive industry.

The brand's future models will focus on adapting to evolving societal needs, emphasizing sustainability, digital transformation, and user-centric design. By embracing collaboration and change, Lotus aims to remain competitive and relevant in an ever-evolving market, setting benchmarks for future electric vehicles while maintaining its heritage of excellence in engineering and design.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Strengths 
summary: The company is strategically positioned in the luxury Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) market, leveraging its status as an early mover to capitalize on the rapid growth of this segment. It offers a diverse portfolio of BEV models priced between $80,000 and $149,000, which represents the largest volume contributor to the luxury BEV market, currently characterized as underserved and ripe for expansion.

With over 70 years of heritage in sports car design and engineering, the Lotus brand is renowned for its iconic status and racing pedigree, having secured multiple Formula 1 championships. The brand's excellence has been recognized with accolades such as "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the 2019 Luxury Briefing Awards and the 2020 MUSE Global Design Award for its Evija model.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Eletre 
summary: The Lotus Eletre is an innovative pure electric SUV that marks the beginning of a new line of lifestyle vehicles, setting a high standard for future models. With a spacious five-seater layout (optional four-seater) and a length of 5,103 millimeters, it offers a comfortable ride thanks to its 3,019-millimeter wheelbase. The Eletre comes in three pre-order versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R, each tailored to different preferences. The Eletre R emphasizes speed and control, while the Eletre S prioritizes range and comfort. Both the Eletre and Eletre S feature a powerful 450 kW powertrain, achieving a top speed of 258 km/h and 0-100 km/h acceleration in 4.5 seconds, with a range of 600 km (WLTP). The Eletre R, priced over $130,000, offers a more robust 675 kW dual-speed system, reaching 265 km/h and accelerating from 0 to 100 km/h in about 2.95 seconds, with a torque of 985 Nm.

All versions are equipped with a 112-kilowatt-hour battery that allows for rapid charging from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes. The Eletre also showcases a unique aerodynamic design that enhances performance and efficiency. Inside, it boasts luxury features, including a 15.1-inch OLED screen, a digital passenger display, and an augmented reality head-up display, all supported by Lotus Hyper OS for advanced connectivity and real-time mapping. The vehicle is 5G compatible, enabling over-the-air updates and smartphone connectivity for various features.

Safety is a priority, with a suite of advanced features such as collision mitigation, traffic sign recognition, and lane departure warnings. As of September 30, 2023, the Eletre has received over 8,600 global orders, with expectations of annual sales reaching 40,000 to 50,000 units starting in 2026.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The Eletre is the first of Lotus Technology's new breed of pure electric SUVs. The Eletre is equipped with a 15.1-inch high-definition OLED screen that works in tandem with the digital passenger display and provides access to its advanced infotainment system.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Pioneering Architecture and Chassis Platform 
summary: The Eletre is an advanced electric vehicle that showcases a state-of-the-art 800-volt EPA architecture, integrating a high-voltage power distribution system with a robust construction of aluminum and high tensile steel for enhanced structural integrity. Its sophisticated battery system features intelligent heat management for efficient energy use, supporting all-wheel drive capabilities. With a remarkable top speed of 265 km/h, the Eletre delivers up to 985 Nm of torque and a peak output of 905 hp, thanks to its dual electric motors and a compact three-in-one electric drive system that combines motors, inverters, and transmitters to optimize weight and efficiency.

Designed with a lightweight philosophy, the Eletre's high energy density battery pack strikes an ideal balance between performance and driving range. The vehicle's advanced EPA system enhances stability and precision through technologies such as active stabilizer bars and an active suspension control system, which adjusts height and damping for a blend of comfort and handling. Additionally, the rear-wheel steering system improves maneuverability at low speeds and agility at high speeds by modifying the turning radius. The Lotus Intelligent Dynamic Control System (LIDC) further enhances flexibility and stability on complex road conditions, while the innovative one-box brake system provides a sports car-like feel and improves energy regeneration during braking, optimizing overall vehicle performance.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Eletre is built on an all-new proprietary 800-volt Electric Power Architecture with an integrated, high-voltage power distribution system. This architecture uses aluminum and high tensile steel for optimal structural rigidity. The Electric Power Architecture’s battery system enables an intelligent heat management operating system for maximum energy conservation. Equipped with all-wheel drive and an electric motor, the Eletre can deliver a top speed of 265 km per hour with a maximum of 985 Nm of torque and it can reach up to 905 hp. The Electric Power Architecture adopts a highly adaptable design and inherits Lotus Technology's light-weight philosophy. The high energy density of the battery pack provides the optimal balance between performance and driving range. There are two electric motors, one driving the front wheels and another driving the rear wheels. The three-in-one electric drive system integrates each motor with an inverter and a transmitter, an efficient design that makes the unit smaller and lighter. The Electric Power Architecture enables drivers to enjoy stability, precision, and flexibility. • Stability. Lotus Technology's Electric Power Architecture system is equipped with active stabilizer bar systems to dynamically adjust stabilizer bars under various driving conditions, delivering a sports car-like experience along with premium agility, cornering stability, and controllability. The active suspension control system adjusts the height and damping rate of the suspension system to provide an optimal balance between comfort and handling performance. • Precision. The active kinematics control available in the Electric Power Architecture, implemented by a rear-wheel steering system, will optimize cornering control and agility at all speeds.

title: Eletre 
summary: The Lotus Eletre is a groundbreaking pure electric SUV that marks the beginning of a new line of lifestyle vehicles, setting a standard for future models. With a spacious five-seater design (optional four-seater), it measures 5,103 millimeters in length and boasts a 3,019-millimeter wheelbase for a comfortable ride. The Eletre offers five standard drive modes, allowing for customizable driving experiences through adjustments in steering, suspension, and propulsion strategies. 

Available in three pre-order versions—Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R—each model caters to distinct customer needs. The Eletre and Eletre S feature a single-speed powertrain with 450 kilowatts, a maximum range of 600 kilometers, a top speed of 258 km/h, and a 0-100 km/h acceleration time of 4.5 seconds, while the Eletre R, priced above $130,000, offers a dual-speed system with 675 kilowatts, a range of 490 kilometers, and acceleration in approximately 2.95 seconds. All models are equipped with a 112-kilowatt-hour battery that supports rapid charging.

The Eletre emphasizes aerodynamics with a design that enhances airflow and reduces resistance, contributing to improved performance. Inside, it features a luxurious cabin with a 15.1-inch OLED screen, a digital passenger display, and augmented reality HUD technology. The Lotus Hyper OS powers the advanced digital cockpit, enabling real-time mapping and customizable displays.

With 5G compatibility, the Eletre supports over-the-air updates and offers a smartphone app for remote access to vehicle functionalities. Safety features include collision mitigation, traffic sign recognition, and lane departure warnings. The Emeya boasts impressive performance, accelerating from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.8 seconds. It features supercharging capabilities, allowing for a range of 150 km in five minutes and achieving 80% capacity in 18 minutes with a 350 kW charger. Deliveries of the Emeya commenced in China in March 2024, with European deliveries anticipated in the second half of 2024. The average Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) for the Emeya exceeds $100,000, with expected annual sales of 30,000 to 40,000 units starting in 2028.

Overall, both the Eletre and Emeya highlight Lotus's commitment to innovation in the electric vehicle segment, combining advanced technology, performance, and luxury in their designs.

The Eletre is a pioneering pure electric SUV that sets a new standard for lifestyle vehicles, featuring a spacious interior for five passengers, customizable driving modes, and three distinct versions—Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R—catering to different customer preferences. The Eletre models are equipped with advanced powertrains, impressive ranges, and rapid charging capabilities, alongside a focus on aerodynamics and luxurious interiors enhanced by cutting-edge technology, including a sophisticated infotainment system and 5G connectivity.

In contrast, the Emeya is Lotus's first hyper grand tourer, showcasing a sleek design and high-performance metrics, aimed at redefining the electric vehicle experience. Deliveries of the Emeya began in China in March 2024, with plans for European deliveries later that year, and it also carries a price point exceeding $100,000. Both models reflect Lotus's dedication to eco-friendly practices and advanced technology, while the broader vision for future models emphasizes the integration of sustainable practices and smart technologies to address urban challenges and enhance quality of life.

This architecture uses aluminum and high tensile steel for optimal structural rigidity. The EPA’s battery system enables an intelligent heat management operating system for maximum energy conservation. Equipped with all-wheel drive and an electric motor, the Eletre can deliver a top speed of 265 km per hour with a maximum of 985 Nm of torque and can reach up to 905 hp. The Electric Power Architecture (EPA) adopts a highly adaptable design and inherits Lotus Technology's light-weight philosophy. The high energy density of the battery pack provides the optimal balance between performance and driving range. There are two electric motors, one driving the front wheels and another driving the rear wheels. The three-in-one electric drive system integrates each motor with an inverter and a transmitter, an efficient design that makes the unit smaller and lighter. The EPA enables drivers to enjoy stability, precision, and flexibility. • Stability. Lotus Technology's Electric Power Architecture (EPA) system is equipped with active stabilizer bar systems to dynamically adjust stabilizer bars under various driving conditions, delivering a sports car-like experience along with premium agility, cornering stability, and controllability. The active suspension control system adjusts the height and damping rate of the suspension system to provide an optimal balance between comfort and handling performance. • Precision. The active kinematics control available in the EPA, implemented by a rear-wheel steering system, optimizes cornering control and agility at all speeds.

title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Lotus Overview**

Lotus, established in the U.K. in 1948, is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility, specializing in the design and development of luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic brand. With a rich racing heritage, Lotus has secured 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles, enhancing its reputation in the automotive industry. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, facilitating its transition from a traditional sports car manufacturer to a leader in high-performance battery electric vehicles (BEVs). Geely's support in manufacturing, research and development (R&D), and supply chain management has been instrumental in this transformation.

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is expected to experience significant growth but remains underserved, with only about 10 luxury BEV models currently available compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models. As an early mover in this space, Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, delivering exceptional performance with three available versions, including the Eletre R, which boasts 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Following this, the second model, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024.

Leveraging its storied sports car design heritage, Lotus possesses robust R&D capabilities that provide a competitive advantage, enabling rapid model rollout and scalable production. All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments, and it also ensures access to high-quality components through established supplier relationships.

To enhance customer experience, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 207 stores globally as of June 30, 2024, with plans for further expansion. The company’s global distribution network is supported by a Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK, allowing for efficient marketing and distribution of Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In terms of sales performance, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023 and 4,873 vehicles in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models.

In summary, Lotus is strategically positioned in the growing luxury BEV market, backed by Geely's resources and expertise, with a strong focus on innovation, customer experience, and operational efficiency.

This strategic positioning within the luxury BEV market, combined with our innovative product offerings and robust R&D capabilities, positions us well for future growth and success in the evolving automotive landscape.
